facts.content: When automating text processing, prefer a clear 'Completion/Prompt' step with step-by-step instructions and constraints for non-technical users.
facts.source: ai-history/grok/processed/items-2025-03.json (Automating Task Processing with Make.com and OpenAI)

facts.content: For automated workflows, use explicit tags/markers in messages to make downstream routing and parsing reliable.
facts.source: ai-history/grok/processed/items-2025-03.json (Automating Task Processing with Make.com and OpenAI)

facts.content: If building an agent for non-technical use, start with a concrete trigger (e.g. specific sender email), connect the source (Gmail), and have the agent draft in the user's style.
The /last30days skill was originally designed for Claude Code (terminal workflow), so ensure prompts/instructions are compatible with OpenClaw's skill system when using it here.
